channel.title: Testing on mgalalm - blog
channel.description: Recent content in Testing on mgalalm - blog

item.title: Laravel Testing Mastering Exception Scenarios
item.description:
Laravel Testing: Mastering Exception Scenarios

In Laravel development, testing is paramount for ensuring your application behaves as expected, especially when dealing with exceptions. Whether they’re custom or built-in, exceptions are an integral part of robust error handling. Let’s explore how to effectively test for both custom and non-custom exceptions in Laravel.

Understanding Exceptions in Laravel

Exceptions in Laravel fall into two categories:

  1. Non-Custom Exceptions: These are PHP’s built-in exceptions like Exception, InvalidArgumentException, etc., which Laravel extends or uses.

item.title: Mastering Laravel Testing Turbocharge Your Code Coverage With Xdebug
item.description: In the dynamic world of Laravel development, ensuring your code is well-tested isn’t just good practice; it’s essential for creating robust applications. Code coverage serves as a critical metric to assess the extent to which your tests cover your codebase. This guide will walk you through setting up Xdebug for code coverage in Laravel, along with troubleshooting common issues. When you first attempt to run:

item.title: Laravel Seeders and Pest: A Casual Look
item.description:
Let’s dive into the world of Laravel seeders used with Pest.

Setting Up Data with Seeders

Here’s a snippet of how you might use a seeder:

item.title: Testing with Fake HTTP Requests in Laravel
item.description:
Testing with Fake HTTP Requests in Laravel

In Laravel, one of the powerful tools for testing is the ability to simulate HTTP responses without actually hitting an external API. This is particularly useful when you need to ensure your application behaves correctly regardless of the API’s response. Here’s how you can use Http::fake() to test a scenario where you’re checking the stock status and price of an item from an Amazon-like service.

item.title: Testing Laravel Applications with Pest
item.description:
Testing Laravel Applications with Pest

Testing is a critical part of software development, ensuring that applications behave as expected. Laravel, known for its elegant syntax and developer-friendly features, has been paired beautifully with Pest, an innovative testing framework for PHP. Here’s how to leverage Pest for writing tests in Laravel.
